topics.topic_en: Constitutional Amendment Blocked by PPP Boycott
topics.summary: On May 7, 2026, the National Assembly voted on a constitutional amendment for the first time in 39 years — incorporating stronger martial-law controls and references to the Gwangju and Buma uprisings — but the vote failed to reach quorum after all 106 PPP lawmakers boycotted the session. National Assembly Speaker Woo Won-shik scheduled a re-vote for May 8, but PPP announced a filibuster, effectively killing the initiative before the June 3 local elections.

evidence_headlines.title: Constitutional revision drive stalls as PPP boycotts Assembly vote
Opposition boycotts 'indisputable' amendment to Constitution
39년 만의 개헌안, 與 주도 다시 처리 시도…국힘 '필리버스터' 예고

topics.topic_en: June 3 Local Elections Campaign Enters Final Month
topics.summary: With 27 days remaining until the June 3 nationwide local elections, polling shows DPK holding a dominant lead — party approval at 46% versus PPP's 18% — while President Lee Jae-myung's approval sits at 67%. Competitive races are emerging in traditionally conservative regions such as Daegu and South Gyeongsang, and 14 simultaneous National Assembly by-elections are drawing added attention.

evidence_headlines.title: 6·3 지방선거가 27일 앞으로 다가온 가운데 더불어민주당 지지율이 46%, 국민의힘 지지율이 18%로 집계
[JTBC 여론조사] 대구시장 선거 '김부겸·추경호' 초접전…경남도 오차범위내
2026 South Korean local elections

topics.topic_en: Han Duck-soo Appeals Sentence Reduced to 15 Years
topics.summary: Seoul High Court on May 7 reduced former Prime Minister Han Duck-soo's prison sentence from 23 years to 15 years on charges of participating in the December 2024 martial law insurrection, ruling that while he helped create a procedural veneer of legitimacy for the unlawful declaration, evidence of active participation was limited. The ruling is expected to serve as a benchmark for appeals by other martial-law co-defendants.

evidence_headlines.title: "내란 적극 가담 안해"…한덕수, 2심서 징역 15년으로 감형
South Korean court reduces prison sentence for ex-prime minister in martial law case
한덕수 2심 징역 15년 선고…1심보다 형량 8년 줄어

topics.topic_en: Yoon Suk-yeol Insurrection Trial Proceedings Continue
topics.summary: Former President Yoon Suk-yeol, already sentenced to life imprisonment for insurrection (February 2026) and an additional seven years for resisting arrest (April 2026 appeals court), faces another trial sentencing on May 28. Ongoing trial coverage keeps the December 2024 martial law crisis at the center of political discourse heading into the local elections.

evidence_headlines.title: Yoon Suk Yeol: Former South Korean President handed life sentence for leading insurrection
Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol gets 7 more years in prison from Seoul appeals court
윤석열 내란 재판/전개

topics.topic_en: North Korea Formalizes Two-State Doctrine in Constitution
topics.summary: North Korea's revised constitution, passed at the March 2026 Supreme People's Assembly and publicly analyzed on May 6, formally deletes the term "reunification" and adds a territorial clause defining DPRK territory as the land north of the armistice line, cementing the "two hostile states" doctrine as state law. Analysts note that exclusion of explicitly adversarial language toward the South leaves limited room for President Lee Jae-myung's peace-coexistence policy.

evidence_headlines.title: 헌법에 '통일' 지운 北… '두 국가' 체제 명문화
북한 '두 국가' 개헌‥통일 '삭제'·영토 '신설'
북한 두 국가 개헌: 통일 삭제, 영토 규정 신설로 남북 관계 새 국면 돌입

topics.topic_en: PPP Internal Fragmentation Ahead of Local Elections
topics.summary: The People Power Party enters the June 3 elections deeply divided: leader Jang Dong-hyeok is trying to distance the party from Yoon while facing internal revolt from supporters of expelled former leader Han Dong-hoon, whose backers held street protests calling for Jang's resignation. With party approval at historic lows around 18%, the party faces potential historic defeats in traditionally safe seats.

evidence_headlines.title: Internal rift deepening in main opposition party over move to expel ex-leader Han Dong-hoon
PPP's Chief Holds Meetings in Washington While His Party Faces Ruin at Home
국민의힘/2026년

topics.topic_en: Korea-US Tariff Deal Uncertainty After IEEPA Ruling
topics.topic_ko: 미국 IEEPA 관세 판결 이후 한미 통상 불확실성
topics.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court's ruling striking down Trump's IEEPA-based reciprocal tariffs has thrown Korea's previously negotiated trade package — a $350 billion U.S. investment pledge in exchange for a 15% tariff rate — into legal uncertainty. Analysts say the near-term market impact on Korea is limited since the 15% blanket rate remains in effect via other statutory authority, but the long-term trade architecture is being renegotiated.

evidence_headlines.title: US Supreme Court strikes down Trump tariffs, clouding Korea trade deal
Survival in the New Age of Trade: What the Supreme Court Ruling on IEEPA Tariffs Means for South Korea
South Korea commits $350 billion to US strategic industries following Trump tariff pressure

topics.topic_en: Lee Government Housing Market Policy Under Scrutiny
topics.summary: President Lee Jae-myung reaffirmed on May 6 that normalizing the overheated real estate market is a top policy priority, with the Blue House reviewing differentiated property tax reforms targeting multi-unit owners and ultra-luxury homes. Critics argue the government has issued only reactive "measures" rather than a coherent long-term policy, and the Ministry of Land is seen as sidelined on the issue.

evidence_headlines.title: Lee reaffirms commitment to curbing overheated housing market
The Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport looking only at the President's 'X'··· "Only real estate 'measures,' no 'policy'"
지방선거 여론조사 – 부동산 세제 합리화 방안 검토

method_notes: Captured via Anthropic API (Sonnet 4.6) with web_search for live Korean political coverage. Topic labels (topic_en) are stable handles for cross-snapshot rank comparison.
